title: lupinus concinnus subsp. optatus
intro: Lupinus concinnus subsp. optatus, commonly known as the Yellow Bush Lupine, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential as a treatment for respiratory issues, such as coughs and congestion, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lupinus concinnus subsp. optatus include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of forms.
